Ghost Shadows.description: Chinese American street gang prominent in New York City's Chinatown from the early 1970s to the mid 1990s. Formed in 1971 by immigrants from Taiwan and Hong Kong, affiliated with the On Leong Tong. Engaged in extortion, kidnapping, murder, racketeering, drug trafficking, and illegal gambling. Became defunct due to federal RICO crackdowns in the 1990s.

Clan Galasso di Poggiomarino.description: The Clan Galasso di Poggiomarino was a Camorra clan operating in the hinterland east of Naples, based in Poggiomarino. Led by Pasquale Galasso and his brother Martino Galasso, it was considered one of the most powerful and ferocious clans in Camorra history. It controlled neighboring municipalities and most public contracts in the province of Naples, with extensions into the province of Salerno. The clan declined in the 1990s after Pasquale Galasso's arrest in 1992 and subsequent collaboration with justice.

Licciardi clan.description: The Clan Licciardi is a powerful Camorra syndicate based in the Secondigliano district of Naples, Italy. It was formed in the mid-1980s by Gennaro Licciardi, who transformed the area into a strategic hub for drug storage and trafficking. After his death, leadership passed to his siblings, notably Maria Licciardi. The clan is a member of the Alleanza di Secondigliano cartel and remains active in drug trafficking and counterfeiting, with operations extending to the Netherlands.

Kkangpae.description: Kkangpae is a Korean term for gangsters or thugs, often referring to unorganized street gangs, while organized crime groups are called geondal or jopok. The Korean mafia may have originated in the late Joseon dynasty, with modern history divided into colonial, political mob, civil war, and present eras. Notable figures include Kim Du-han, who unified Korean mobs and fought the yakuza during Japanese colonial rule.

Ghetto Brothers.description: The Ghetto Brothers were a gang and music group founded in New York City's South Bronx in the late 1960s. They aimed to uplift young Latino and Black men in their community and were involved in politics, particularly the Puerto Rican independence movement. The gang spread to much of the Northeastern United States and later merged with the Savage Nomads to form Los Solidos in the 1990s.

'Ndrina Albanese.description: The Albanese are a powerful 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cittanova and Laureana di Borrello, Calabria, Italy. They have branches in Lombardy, Tuscany, Lazio, and Liguria, and are allied with the Raso-Gullace clan. Their activities include drug trafficking, grape market control, and past kidnappings. In 2016, the Alchemia operation led to 40 arrests for mafia association, corruption, and bid-rigging.
